qualifying_org_definition: Any public or private nonprofit agency, church or other religious group, community organization, or governmental agency. This is verbatim from NC FNS-260 §260.04.B.3.c(i) (November 26, 2025): 'Accept volunteer work for any nonprofit, churches or other religious groups, community organizations, or governmental agency, or community service ordered by a court.' No pre-approval, MOU, or worksite agreement is required for the general volunteer lane — those apply only to the optional MTAJ-NC E&T track under §260.09.D.
verification_instrument: Written or verbal verification from the supervising organization confirming the number of hours volunteered in a month. This is stated verbatim in NC FNS-260 §260.04.B.3.c(ii): 'Request written or verbal verification from the agency of the number of hours volunteered in a month.' No named state form is required; a letter or phone confirmation from the org is sufficient. DSS-8569 (Consolidated Work Notice) is the standard general work-registration notice issued to recipients. DSS-6905 (Work Experience/Community Service Agreement) applies only to formal MTAJ-NC E&T track enrollment and is not a prerequisite for general volunteer-hour credit.
hours_formula_detail: NC FNS-260 §260.04.B.3 states the ABAWD work requirement is met by 'Working an average of 20 hours per week (80 hours in the month)' for the general volunteer/work lane. A separate workfare formula (benefit allotment ÷ $7.25 federal minimum wage) applies only to the formal community work experience (E&T work site) track under §260.09.C, not to the general volunteer lane. For the general lane Tended operates in, 80 hours/month is the applicable threshold.
accepts_outofstate_detail: NC FNS-260 §260.04.B.3.c(i) says to accept volunteer work 'for any nonprofit, churches or other religious groups, community organizations, or governmental agency' with no geographic or in-state restriction. The policy is silent on whether the nonprofit must be NC-registered or physically located in NC. Under federal default (7 CFR 273.24), any qualifying nonprofit may verify volunteer hours. No NC-specific restriction was found in FNS-260 (Nov 2025), FNS-265, or any secondary source. Out-of-state 501(c)(3)s should be accepted operationally under the federal default absent explicit NC restriction.
geographic_waivers: none — fully enforced statewide. NC had no active ABAWD area waiver as of FY2024 Q4. The OBBBA (H.R.1, signed July 4, 2025) eliminated the ABAWD area waiver authority entirely; as of 2026 no county-level waivers exist or are possible under federal law.
submission_deadline: by the 10th of the month following the month in which work hours were reduced below 80/month (per NC FNS-260 §260.04.D). Ongoing monthly compliance is tracked by county DSS through NC FAST. No specific deadline is stated for affirmative monthly hour submissions; the 10th-of-following-month deadline is framed as the reportable-change deadline when hours drop.
notes:
NC FNS 260 policy (updated Nov 26, 2025) states that if an ABAWD volunteers at a nonprofit, the work 'may qualify as a self-initiated community work experience work site,' but immediately adds that 'for the work site to qualify as a More Than a Job NC work site, the agency must be under a work site agreement with the local department of social services' — form DSS-6905. The worksite-agreement language is framed around the MTAJ-NC E&T program track, creating ambiguity about whether a pure general-volunteer lane (no county agreement) is operationally available; in practice county DSS workers may require or default to the DSS-6905 agreement. The FNS-260 PDFs on policies.ncdhhs.gov return HTTP 403, so full policy text could not be directly verified; the critical quoted language was retrieved via Google snippet indexing of those documents. Confidence is medium: a general lane may exist in federal regs but NC appears to operationalize volunteer credit through a county worksite-agreement channel.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-20): Additional research including the full NC SNAP E&T State Plan FY24 (DocuSign Envelope 12DC0625, fetched from ncdhhs.gov) and the More Than A Job NC Program Handbook (Revised 2024) resolves the original ambiguity in favor of a general volunteer lane with no MOU prestep. Key findings: (1) NC's E&T program is ALL-VOLUNTARY (Section V of the State Plan: 'Voluntary per 7 CFR 273.7(e)(5)(i)'); NC does not operate workfare and did not pledge to serve all at-risk ABAWDs. (2) The formal E&T program (MTAJ-NC) operates in approximately 30 of 100 counties; in the remaining ~70 counties there is no formal E&T track at all, meaning ABAWDs in those counties can only satisfy the time limit through self-initiated work/volunteer activity — confirming a general lane must exist independent of DSS-6905. (3) The MTAJ-NC handbook's 'Worksite Development and Management' function (SIS code 582) is explicitly about identifying E&T program worksites for the formal MTAJ-NC track, not about approving sites for general ABAWD compliance. (4) DSS-8569 (Consolidated Work Notice) is the standard general work-registration form; DSS-6905 is specific to the formal E&T track. The original record's required_prestep='MOU' was a misread of the FNS-265 language: 'for the work site to qualify as a More Than a Job NC work site' means for the formal E&T program track, not for general ABAWD time-limit compliance. Adversarial search found no NC pre-approved volunteer site list, no mandatory caseworker-approval step for the general lane, and no third-party sources indicating a DSS-6905 is required outside MTAJ-NC. Tier revised from 2_hoops to 1_clean; general_volunteer_lane changed from unknown to yes; required_prestep changed from MOU to none. Confidence remains medium (not high) because the FNS-260 primary source document is still inaccessible (HTTP 403) and caseworker discretion in non-E&T counties cannot be ruled out from public sources alone.

FINAL PASS (2026-06-21): Successfully downloaded and extracted full text of NC FNS-260 (November 26, 2025 update, 33 pages) directly from policies.ncdhhs.gov — the HTTP 403 barrier that blocked prior passes was circumvented with a browser User-Agent string. Also extracted NC FNS-265 (ABAWD Services Program, September 20, 2021, 2 pages). Key primary-source findings that resolve all remaining ambiguity: (1) §260.04.B.3.c states EXPLICITLY: 'Volunteer work in a public or private organization. (i) Accept volunteer work for any nonprofit, churches or other religious groups, community organizations, or governmental agency, or community service ordered by a court. (ii) Request written or verbal verification from the agency of the number of hours volunteered in a month.' This is the general lane — verified straight from policy, no ambiguity. (2) The worksite-agreement language (DSS-6905) appears ONLY in §260.09.D under 'Self-initiated Community Work Experience (work sites)' — which is the MTAJ-NC formal E&T track subsection. The plain-language reading: a volunteer who reports hours directly to their caseworker qualifies under §260.04 without any worksite agreement; the DSS-6905 is only needed if the county wants to formally enroll the volunteer site into the MTAJ-NC program for E&T reimbursement purposes. (3) The FNS-265 document (§265.01.D) uses identical language, confirming the worksite-agreement requirement is scoped to 'E&T work site' status, not general ABAWD compliance. (4) No named verification form exists for the general volunteer lane — 'written or verbal verification from the agency' (org letter or phone confirmation) is the stated standard. (5) The policy document is silent on remote/online work — no restriction or explicit allowance found; classified as 'silent'. Confidence upgraded to HIGH: the primary source (NC FNS-260, Nov 2025) was fully read and directly confirms general_volunteer_lane=yes, required_prestep=none, qualifying_org_definition (any nonprofit/church/community org/gov agency), verification_instrument (written or verbal org confirmation), and hours_reporting=self_reported. Multiple independent secondary sources agree. Adversarial check found no contradicting language. county_variation_flag remains true because MTAJ-NC formal track is only in ~30 of 100 counties, so caseworker practices may vary in non-E&T counties, though the policy standard is clear.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): Re-downloaded NC FNS-260 (Nov 2025) via curl with browser UA and extracted full text. (1) HOURS FORMULA: The general volunteer/work lane uses 80 hours/month (federal standard) per §260.04.B.3 — 'Working an average of 20 hours per week (80 hours in the month).' The workfare formula (benefit ÷ $7.25) applies only to the formal E&T community work experience track (§260.09.C) and is not the threshold for the general volunteer lane Tended uses. Set monthly_hours_required=80, hours_formula=federal_80. (2) OUT-OF-STATE NONPROFIT: FNS-260 says 'any nonprofit' with no geographic restriction. Policy is silent on location of the certifying org — no in-state requirement found anywhere in FNS-260, FNS-265, or secondary sources. Set accepts_outofstate_nonprofit=silent. (3) GEOGRAPHIC WAIVERS: NC had no active area waivers as of FY2024. OBBBA eliminated waiver authority entirely as of July 4, 2025. Fully enforced statewide. (4) SUBMISSION MECHANISM: Recipients bring/mail/phone verification from the org to their county DSS worker; the caseworker enters it into NC FAST. No online recipient portal for hour submission found. Set submission_mechanism=[paper_county_office], noting that mail and phone are also used (verbal verification is explicitly permitted). (5) REPORTING CADENCE/DEADLINE: FNS-260 §260.04.D states ABAWDs 'are required to report when they experience a reduction in work hours below 20 hours per week or 80 hours a month by the 10th of the month following the month the work hours were reduced.' This is framed as a change-reporting deadline, not a regular monthly submission deadline. Ongoing compliance appears tracked at recertification and when changes occur. Set reporting_cadence=monthly, submission_deadline='by the 10th of the month following the month in which hours reduced below threshold (change-reporting rule); ongoing hour submissions tracked by county DSS in NC FAST.' (6) FORM FIELDS: No named NC form for the general volunteer lane. Reasonable default baseline applied.
